title: Contributing to CatastRo
text: Fork package clone onto computer. haven’t done , recommend using usethis::create_from_github("rOpenSpain/CatastRo", fork = TRUE). Install development dependencies devtools::install_dev_deps(), make sure package passes R CMD check running devtools::check(). R CMD check doesn’t pass cleanly, ’s good idea ask help continuing. Create Git branch pull request (PR). recommend using usethis::pr_init("brief-description--change"). Make changes, commit git, create PR running usethis::pr_push(), following prompts browser. title PR briefly describe change. body PR contain Fixes #issue-number. user-facing changes, add bullet top NEWS.md (.e. just first header). Follow style described https://style.tidyverse.org/news.html.

title: Get started
text: OVCCoordenadas service retrieves coordinates known cadastral reference (geocoding). can also retrieve cadastral references near pair coordinates (reverse geocoding). CatastRo returns results tibbles. operations described detail corresponding vignette (see vignette("ovcservice", package = "CatastRo")).

title: Get started
text: INSPIRE Directive aims create European Union Spatial Data Infrastructure (SDI) purposes EU environmental policies policies activities may impact environment. European Spatial Data Infrastructure enables sharing environmental spatial information among public sector organizations, facilitates public access spatial information across Europe assist policy-making across boundaries. Source: INSPIRE Knowledge Base Spanish Cadastre implementation INSPIRE directive (see Spanish Cadastre INSPIRE services) provides access spatial objects cadastral database: Vector objects: Parcels, addresses, buildings, cadastral zones . CatastRo returns objects sf objects, using sf package. Imagery: Image layers representing information vector objects. CatastRo returns objects SpatRaster objects, using terra package. Note services cover 95% Spanish territory, excluding Basque Country Navarre1, independent cadastral offices. three INSPIRE services: ATOM service: ATOM service downloads complete municipal datasets different cadastral elements. WFS service: WFS service downloads vector objects specific cadastral elements. Note restrictions extent number elements can queried. full municipal downloads, prefer ATOM service. WMS service: WMS service downloads georeferenced map images different cadastral elements.

title: Get started
text: example demonstrates main capabilities package recreating cadastral map surroundings Santiago Bernabéu Stadium. use WMS WFS services retrieve different layers. Figure 1: Santiago Bernabéu example can also create thematic maps attributes spatial objects. example visualizes urban growth Granada CatastRo, reproducing map Dominic Royé (Royé 2019) ATOM service. First, retrieve geometry Granada’s city center mapSpain: Next, use catr_get_code_from_coords() identify Granada’s cadastral municipality code, download buildings catr_atom_get_buildings(). Next, limit analysis circle radius 1.5 km around city center: Figure 2: Minimal cadastral map Granada Next, extract construction year beginning column: Finally, group data construction year create visualization. cut() function creates classes decade 1900 onward: Figure 3: Granada - Urban growth
code: # Extract buildings by bounding box. # Check https://boundingbox.klokantech.com/  library(CatastRo)  stadium <- catr_wfs_get_buildings_bbox(   c(-3.6891446916, 40.4523311971, -3.687462138, 40.4538643165),   srs = 4326 )  # Extract cadastral parcels using a spatial object as the query input.  stadium_parcel <- catr_wfs_get_parcels_bbox(stadium)  # Transform to the tile CRS.  stadium_parcel_pr <- sf::st_transform(stadium_parcel, 25830)  # Extract parcel label imagery.  labs <- catr_wms_get_layer(   stadium_parcel_pr,   what = "parcel",   styles = "BoundariesOnly",   srs = 25830 )  # Plot. library(ggplot2) library(tidyterra) # Plot SpatRaster layers.  ggplot() +   geom_spatraster_rgb(data = labs) +   geom_sf(     data = stadium_parcel_pr,     fill = NA, col = "red", linewidth = 2   ) +   geom_sf(data = stadium, fill = "red", alpha = .5) +   coord_sf(crs = 25830) library(dplyr) library(sf) library(mapSpain)  # Use esp_get_capimun() to get the city geometry.  city <- esp_get_capimun(munic = "^Granada$") city_catr_code <- catr_get_code_from_coords(city)  city_catr_code #> # A tibble: 1 × 12 #>   munic   catr_to catr_munic catrcode cpro  cmun  inecode nm      cd    cmc   cp    cm    #>   <chr>   <chr>   <chr>      <chr>    <chr> <chr> <chr>   <chr>   <chr> <chr> <chr> <chr> #> 1 GRANADA 18      900        18900    18    087   18087   GRANADA 18    900   18    87  city_bu <- catr_atom_get_buildings(city_catr_code$catrcode) buff <- city |>   # Adjust CRS to 25830 for buildings.   st_transform(st_crs(city_bu)) |>   # Buffer.   st_buffer(1500)  # Cut buildings. dataviz <- st_intersection(city_bu, buff)  ggplot(dataviz) +   geom_sf() # Extract the first four positions. year <- substr(dataviz$beginning, 1, 4)  # Replace entries that do not look like years with 0000. year[!(year %in% 0:2500)] <- "0000"  # Convert to numeric. year <- as.integer(year)  # Add a new column. dataviz <- dataviz |>   mutate(year = year) dataviz <- dataviz |>   mutate(     year_cat = cut(year, breaks = c(0, seq(1900, 2030, by = 10)), dig.lab = 4)   )  ggplot(dataviz) +   geom_sf(aes(fill = year_cat), color = NA, na.rm = TRUE) +   scale_fill_manual(     values = hcl.colors(15, "Spectral"),     na.translate = FALSE   ) +   theme_void() +   labs(title = "GRANADA", fill = "") +   theme(     panel.background = element_rect(fill = "black"),     plot.background = element_rect(fill = "black"),     legend.justification = .5,     legend.text = element_text(       colour = "white",       size = 12     ),     plot.title = element_text(       colour = "white",       hjust = .5,       margin = margin(t = 30),       size = 30     ),     plot.caption = element_text(       colour = "white",       margin = margin(b = 20),       hjust = .5     ),     plot.margin = margin(r = 40, l = 40)   )

title: OVCCoordenadas service
text: OVCCoordenadas service available following functions: catr_ovc_get_rccoor(). catr_ovc_get_rccoor_distancia(). catr_ovc_get_cpmrc().

title: OVCCoordenadas service
text: catr_ovc_get_rccoor() takes coordinates (lat lon) spatial reference system (srs) used express . returns tibble cadastral reference property spatial point, including information address (town, street number). function accepts following values srs argument: can retrieve cadastral references within 50 square meters lat lon catr_ovc_get_rccoor_distancia().
code: library(CatastRo)  result <- catr_ovc_get_rccoor(   lat = 38.6196566583596,   lon = -3.45624183836806,   srs = "4230" )  knitr::kable(result) data(catr_srs_values)  # OVC valid codes. library(dplyr)  catr_srs_values |>   filter(ovc_service == TRUE) |>   select(SRS, Description) |>   knitr::kable() catr_ovc_get_rccoor_distancia(   lat = 40.96002,   lon = -5.663408,   srs = "4230" ) |>   knitr::kable()

title: OVCCoordenadas service
text: opposite query, catr_ovc_get_cpmrc() accepts cadastral reference (rc) returns xcoord ycoord specified srs, together address. Optional province municipality values can narrow search. following query narrows search municipality: can also query rc:
code: catr_ovc_get_cpmrc(   rc = "13077A01800039",   srs = "4230",   province = "CIUDAD REAL",   municipality = "SANTA CRUZ DE MUDELA" ) |>   knitr::kable() catr_ovc_get_cpmrc(   rc = "13077A01800039",   municipality = "SANTA CRUZ DE MUDELA" ) |>   knitr::kable() #> ✖ OVC service error "11": LA PROVINCIA ES OBLIGATORIA # Get the result without a warning. catr_ovc_get_cpmrc(rc = "13077A01800039") |>   knitr::kable()

title: CatastRo
text: Install CatastRo CRAN: SSL certificate Spanish Cadastre may cause errors using CatastRo (especially macOS, see issue #40). CatastRo >= 1.0.0, can work around errors running code loading package: make setting persistent, add code .Rprofile:
code: install.packages("CatastRo") # Disable SSL verification options(catastro_ssl_verify = 0) .Rprofile # ... other options... options(catastro_ssl_verify = 0)

title: CatastRo
text: OVC services use OVCCoordenadas geocoding reverse geocoding OVCCallejero province municipality code lookup. functions use catr_ovc_get_*() prefix return tibbles tibble package. See vignette("ovcservice", package = "CatastRo") detailed description functions.

title: CatastRo
text: INSPIRE functions retrieve spatial objects Spanish Cadastre INSPIRE services using sf terra packages. Note services cover 95% Spanish territory, excluding Basque Country Navarre1, independent cadastral offices. three INSPIRE services:

title: CatastRo
text: ATOM service downloads complete municipal datasets different cadastral elements. Results returned sf objects sf package. functions use catr_atom_get_*() prefix.

title: CatastRo
text: WFS service downloads vector objects specific cadastral elements. Results returned sf objects sf package. Note restrictions extent number elements can queried. full municipal downloads, prefer ATOM service. functions use catr_wfs_get_*() prefix.

title: CatastRo
text: WMS service downloads georeferenced map images different cadastral elements. Results returned SpatRaster objects terra package. single function querying service: catr_wms_get_layer().

title: Get the cadastral municipality code from coordinates — catr_get_code_from_coords
text: x Coordinate input. can : pair coordinates c(x, y). case srs coordinates must provided. sf object. object several geometries, first geometry used. function extracts coordinates using sf::st_centroid(x, of_largest_polygon = TRUE). srs SRS/CRS use query. see allowed values, use catr_srs_values, specifically ovc_service column. verbose Logical. TRUE, displays informational messages. cache_dir Path cache directory. NULL, function stores cached files temporary directory. See base::tempdir(). ... Arguments passed mapSpain::esp_get_munic_siane year Character string number. Release year. must use format YYYY (assuming end year) YYYY-MM-DD. Historical information starts 2005. resolution Character string number. Resolution geospatial data. One : "10": 1:10 million. "6.5": 1:6.5 million. "3": 1:3 million. region Optional. vector region names, NUTS ISO codes (see esp_dict_region_code()). munic Character string. name regex expression names required municipalities. Use NULL return municipalities.

title: Set your CatastRo cache directory — catr_set_cache_dir
text: Source files cached download. CatastRo implements following caching options: occasional use, rely default tempdir()-based cache (install). Modify cache single session setting catr_set_cache_dir(cache_dir = "/path/"). reproducible workflows, install persistent cache catr_set_cache_dir(cache_dir = "/path/", install = TRUE). cache kept across R sessions. caching specific files, use cache_dir argument corresponding function. Cached files can occasionally become corrupt. case, try downloading data setting update_cache = TRUE corresponding function. download fails, try another download method save file cache_dir. Use verbose = TRUE inspect API query catr_detect_cache_dir() identify cache path.
